Runtime Foundation MVP-51 runtime planning track

Runtime planning track

Runtime Foundation

This page explains the path from static demo to safe runtime planning. It does not turn on live execution. It defines the controls that must exist before any real action can run.

MVP-51 Runtime activation has not started. Live runtime agents enabled: 0. Static, read-only, in-browser

What stays disabled

  • No commands execute from this page.
  • No backend writes are enabled.
  • No Supabase mutations are enabled.
  • No automation is enabled.
  • No rollback execution is enabled.
  • No Netlify functions or endpoints are added.

What MVP-51 is

Runtime foundation overview

What MVP-51 is

A planning and static-readiness pass that defines the runtime gates, schemas, contracts, and review steps needed before any live execution can ever be considered.

What MVP-51 is not

It is not runtime activation. It does not run agents. It does not mutate data. It does not add endpoints or functions.

Required controls

Authenticated user, tenant/workspace scope, role permission, registered action, risk classification, dry-run result, approval decision, audit event, queue record if required, rollback or incident readiness note, and human review if required.

Runtime ladder

From static demo to controlled pilot

  1. Static demo
  2. Runtime foundation blueprint
  3. Auth/RBAC prototype
  4. Tenant model
  5. Audit ledger
  6. Action registry
  7. Approval gates
  8. Dry-run engine
  9. Queue / human review
  10. Private sandbox with fake data
  11. Limited controlled pilot

Activation prerequisites

Controls that must exist before any real action can run

Required schemas

Role, tenant workspace, action registry, approval gate, audit event, dry-run result, and runtime activation gate schemas.

Safety contracts

Non-execution, dry-run-before-execution, human approval, and audit-everything contracts.

Enterprise checklist

Review auth/RBAC, boundaries, audit, approvals, dry-run, queueing, monitoring, incident planning, security, legal, and explicit activation approval.

Required schemas

Static schema references

user_role.schema.json

owner_admin, system_admin, security_reviewer, approver, operator, stakeholder_viewer, audit_viewer, developer, disabled_user.

tenant_workspace.schema.json

tenant_id, tenant_name, workspace_id, environment, data_boundary, allowed_users, audit_scope, runtime_enabled false by default.

action_registry.schema.json

action_id, risk_level, required_role, dry_run_required, human_review_required, rollback_required, execution_enabled false by default.

approval_gate.schema.json

approval_id, approver, approval_status, required_approval_level, denial_reason, expires_at.

audit_event.schema.json

audit_event_id, event_type, approval_status, dry_run_status, execution_status, runtime_enabled_at_event_time.

dry_run_result.schema.json

dry_run_id, approval_required, rollback_notes, dry_run_status, execution_allowed false by default.

runtime_activation_gate.schema.json

runtime_gate_id, gate_name, required_status, current_status, blocking_reason, checked_by, checked_at.

Safety contracts

Non-execution rules

Non-execution contract

MVP-51 does not enable runtime. Any implementation that enables execution, writes, endpoint mutation, queue processing, automation, or rollback is out of scope and must fail validation.

Dry-run before execution

No real action may execute without a completed dry-run result, risk classification, audit record, and approval decision.

Human approval

High-risk actions require human approval. Deploy, rollback, database mutation, alert sending, and external API mutation cannot be executed by default.

Audit everything

Every request, classification, approval, denial, dry run, queue transition, and execution decision must create an audit event before any future runtime activation.

Enterprise runtime checklist

What still blocks runtime

Auth/RBAC

Authenticated user and role validation must exist.

Tenant/workspace

Boundaries must be defined and enforced.

Audit ledger

Every decision must be auditable.

Approval gates

High-risk actions need human approval.

Dry-run engine

Real execution requires a completed dry run first.

Queue / human review

Queued and reviewed states are required where appropriate.

Monitoring / readiness

Monitoring and readiness notes must exist before activation.

Incident / rollback planning

Planning notes exist; execution remains disabled.

Security / legal review

Ownership, security, and legal review must be explicit.

Pilot scope

Only a narrow private sandbox with fake data can be considered first.

Activation approval

Explicit approval is required before any future runtime activation.

Status

Runtime activation has not started. Live runtime agents enabled: 0.

What remains disabled

Hard boundaries

  • No commands execute from this page.
  • No backend writes are enabled.
  • No Supabase mutations are enabled.
  • No automation is enabled.
  • No rollback execution is enabled.
  • No endpoints or Netlify functions are added.
